events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are tasked to evaluate a field condition for Lighting controls & emergency egress. The scenario: commercial construction, electrical trade, tested/inspected stage. The field condition is compared with referenced drawing or manufacturer sheet, and tags, ratings, layout callouts line up with installed work, revision block is current. Reference materials: NEC 700, NFPA 101. The prompt asks to evaluate if this passes, fails, or needs more info. Since the description says everything lines up and is current, that sounds like a pass. There's no mention of any defects. So decision should be "pass". Risk likely "low". Work stage is "tested/inspected". Component conditions: none flagged, so empty list. Percent complete: likely high, maybe 100%? Since tested/inspected, I'd say 100%? But maybe not fully accepted? The stage is tested/inspected, so maybe 100% of the scope? I'll put 100. Value: no numeric reading asked, leave 0. Confidence: high, say 0.95. Event time, rate, sound_source, order, workable: not relevant, leave default. Findings: empty list or maybe positive? "All items match approved submittals and code". Actions: none. Rationale: "Installed lighting controls and emergency egress comply with drawings, tags, ratings, and layout, and are up to date per revision block. No defects observed during testing/inspection." References: NEC 700, NFPA 101. I'll output JSON accordingly.
